page: Amazon Web Services inf2.8xlarge is a virtualized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local NVMe storage or discrete GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the inf2.8xlarge demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, scoring in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Multi-core performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls within the average range. Cryptographic capabilities are a notable strength, with the Passmark CPU encryption test ranking in the top 25%, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AES and VAES. Memory performance is also robust, with the Passmark memory score placing in the top 25%, though large-block memory read and write bandwidth benchmarks remain in the average tier.

For specialized workloads, the instance shows strong performance in text generation for smaller language models, such as the 135M parameter model, while prompt processing and larger model execution perform at average levels. Given the balance of 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high single-core throughput and strong cryptographic execution without the need for local storage. Suitable workloads include general-purpose compute, web serving, database operations, and moderate machine learning inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services inf2.8xlarge is a Nitro-based virtual server powered by a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores. It features 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps, and no local storage or discrete GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution and high cryptographic throughput, alongside strong memory performance. Multi-core processing and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the average tier. The instance is highly capable at text generation for smaller language models and excels in cryptographic tasks. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the inf2.8xlarge is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, database operations, and machine learning inference tasks that do not require local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.8xlarge is a Nitro-based instance featuring a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC CPU, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 17 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong single-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.8xlarge features 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 17 Gbps network speed, offering strong single-core and cryptographic performance.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, strong single-core performance, and robust cryptographic acceleration.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
128.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Strong single-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Optimized for general-purpose compute, database operations, and light LLM inference
